title: drymocallis glandulosa subsp. hansenii
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. hansenii is a plant species that belongs to the rose family and is commonly known as a subspecies of the drymocallis glandulosa, a type of yarrow or buckthorn. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have made it useful for treating wounds, fever, and various other health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce pain, lower fever, and support immune function, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. hansenii can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
